authority_classes.definition: Canonical Athanor implementation and adopted runtime-facing truth.
Devstack design, prototype, proving, and capability-forge truth.
Searchable Claude-era evidence that does not outrank current truth.

capability_stages.definition: Design intent exists but no proof artifact is ready.
Working slice exists but is not yet ready for Athanor adoption.
Capability has concrete evidence and is eligible for a promotion packet.
Capability is represented in Athanor canonical truth and may affect real behavior.
Capability is intentionally superseded and kept only for lineage.

capabilities.label: Agent Gateway Layer
capabilities.runtime_target: VAULT LiteLLM plus Athanor routing surfaces
acceptance_criteria: Athanor registries define canonical provider, routing, and model-lane truth.
Gateway updates land through Athanor control-plane surfaces instead of lingering only in devstack prose.
capabilities.rollback_or_disable_path: Revert the affected Athanor registry and runtime packet changes, then re-run the governed VAULT or vLLM runtime packets.
capabilities.archive_instructions: Archive superseded devstack gateway design notes once Athanor surfaces remain canonical and current.
notes: Devstack remains the proving/configuration lane for future gateway work.
Athanor registries and docs remain the adopted-system truth.

capabilities.label: GraphRAG Hybrid Retrieval
capabilities.runtime_target: Athanor governed knowledge subsystem: FOUNDRY GraphRAG retrieval service plus Athanor agent retrieval integration over the existing truth.docs and truth.graph namespaces
acceptance_criteria: Hybrid retrieval must run against real Athanor and devstack documents with stable proof artifacts.
The adoption target is fixed as a governed knowledge subsystem rather than a free-floating service proof.
Runtime deployment and rollback must land through explicit FOUNDRY GraphRAG and Athanor agents packet linkage before adoption.
approval_gated_remaining: Operator review and packet acceptance remain the only adoption gate; the governed runtime revision is already live and green, so no further compose-recovery or vector-path unblock work remains before widening beyond shadow evidence.
capabilities.rollback_or_disable_path: Disable the GraphRAG knowledge-subsystem delta by reverting Athanor retrieval integration and leaving the devstack proof service isolated behind the foundry-graphrag runtime lane.
capabilities.archive_instructions: Retain proof artifacts in devstack and archive superseded wrapper or integration-shape experiments now that the governed knowledge-subsystem landing zone is selected.
notes: The landing zone is now explicit: a governed knowledge subsystem rooted in the FOUNDRY GraphRAG service and Athanor agents integration surfaces.

The 2026-04-12 governed Foundry compose redeploy is now live, and the promotion eval reran green with valid shadow-tier evidence recorded in C:/Athanor/reports/truth-inventory/graphrag-promotion-eval.json.

The source-side Athanor agents retrieval seam is now landed in C:/Athanor/projects/agents plus the platform-topology service map, so the remaining boundary is governed runtime deployment rather than more design shaping.

The current retrieval revision is now live through the governed foundry-agents-compose-deploy-packet, and the promotion eval reran green with valid shadow-tier evidence recorded in C:/Athanor/reports/truth-inventory/graphrag-promotion-eval.json.
The 2026-04-13 wake-before-embed hardening closed the embedding sleep-state blocker, and the current hybrid proof now passes cleanly on route `hybrid` with valid shadow-tier evidence.

The next adopted delta is packet acceptance and ordinary retrieval quality follow-through; the runtime revision is already live and green, so no more compose-recovery or vector-path unblock work remains.

capabilities.label: Watchdog Runtime Guard
capabilities.runtime_target: Athanor runtime guard and remediation surfaces
acceptance_criteria: Pause, resume, restart, and remediation boundaries must be explicit and operator-safe.
Runtime guard adoption requires a bounded packet and a clean rollback story.
capabilities.rollback_or_disable_path: Leave the capability paused or devstack-only until Athanor-side runtime and approval boundaries are packeted.
capabilities.archive_instructions: Archive superseded MVW iterations after a single Athanor-bound packet path exists.
notes: Runtime experiments are real, but Athanor adoption still needs a packeted safety boundary.

capabilities.label: GPU Scheduler Extension
acceptance_criteria: Scheduler work must extend the existing Athanor GPU orchestrator instead of creating a competing control plane.
Runtime deployment must land through the existing governed packet and lane system.
approval_gated_remaining: Review and accept the promotion packet as the bounded scheduler-extension adoption path before widening beyond shadow evidence.
capabilities.rollback_or_disable_path: Keep the scheduler delta out of Athanor runtime until the packet is accepted; revert to the current orchestrator lane if a packeted rollout fails.
capabilities.archive_instructions: Archive superseded scheduler design deltas once the chosen extension path is adopted.
notes: Extends an already-adopted Athanor surface, so promotion must land as a bounded delta rather than a second scheduler.
The runtime linkage is the existing foundry-gpu-orchestrator deployment lane plus a dedicated scheduler-state rollout packet for the new bounded delta.

The 2026-04-12 rerun now pins the live six-zone orchestrator current state, and live probes show /scheduler/state plus the bounded request/preload/release routes with write-capability posture enabled in runtime.

The bounded scheduler extension now has a valid formal promotion eval, and the canonical scheduler-state rollout packet is backfilled as executed from live evidence, so adoption is now packet-review plus bounded rollout governance rather than more scheduler implementation work.

capabilities.label: Creative Identity Pipeline
acceptance_criteria: Identity-consistent image generation, Drive artifact discipline, and I2V proof must be stable enough to packetize.
The adopted boundary must be clear across EOQ, ComfyUI workflows, and project packets.
capabilities.rollback_or_disable_path: Leave the pipeline in devstack and do not promote it into Athanor runtime surfaces until the proof is stable.
capabilities.archive_instructions: Archive superseded prompt, LoRA, and workflow experiments once a stable Athanor-bound packet exists.
notes: The quality and workflow proof live in devstack first; Athanor adoption should wait for stable packet boundaries.

capabilities.label: Gateway and Agent Onboarding Hardening
capabilities.runtime_target: Athanor gateway onboarding and operator-facing routing surfaces
acceptance_criteria: Gateway alias guidance, onboarding instructions, and health checks must stay aligned with Athanor provider and routing registries.
No secret values may be copied into repo docs or adopted operator surfaces.
The adopted delta must land in Athanor control-plane truth instead of lingering only in devstack notes.
capabilities.rollback_or_disable_path: Keep onboarding and gateway hardening in devstack only until the Athanor packet is accepted; if adoption drifts, revert the Athanor-side registry and docs delta.
capabilities.archive_instructions: Archive superseded onboarding examples and gateway handoff notes after Athanor owns the adopted operator path for this delta.
notes: The adopted gateway layer already exists in Athanor; this tracks the next hardening delta rather than a second gateway.

capabilities.label: Goose Operator Shell
capabilities.runtime_target: Athanor operator shell, dashboard routing recommendations, and shell-level execution policy
acceptance_criteria: Goose is scoped as the primary shell-level operator agent without becoming a second control plane.
Direct CLI, Goose-wrapped, and LiteLLM-routed execution boundaries are explicit.
MCP extension use is bounded by Athanor vendor-policy and sensitivity rules.
Operator-shell recommendations and failure fallbacks are visible in the atlas and dashboard surfaces.
capabilities.rollback_or_disable_path: Remove Goose from the preferred shell path, restore direct terminal plus specialist CLI routing as the default operator path, and keep Goose-specific logic outside Athanor adopted truth.
capabilities.archive_instructions: Archive temporary shell-comparison notes and superseded shell-selection scratch docs once the governed shell policy is adopted or rejected.
notes: Goose is the adopted bounded shell path, not a replacement control plane.
Promotion should stay bounded to orchestration, shell policy, and operator-surface guidance.
The boundary evidence now lives in C:/Athanor/reports/truth-inventory/goose-boundary-evidence.md.
The 2026-04-12 formal shell comparison is green at 8 of 8 once Goose is pinned to the intended LiteLLM DeepSeek lane instead of the weaker workstation-local default model.
The 2026-04-14 standing operator approval closes the remaining packet-review gate and promotes Goose into adopted shell truth.
The governed DESK helper is now the ordinary bounded shell handoff path; future changes stay packet-backed instead of drifting through workstation-local defaults.

capabilities.label: OpenHands Bounded Worker Lane
capabilities.runtime_target: Athanor bounded worker-lane orchestration and governed coding-worker experiments
acceptance_criteria: OpenHands runs only as an explicitly selected worker lane.
Its MCP and tool surfaces remain bounded by Athanor policy and repo sensitivity.
Worker outputs are reproducible, reviewable, and do not accumulate competing control-plane state.
Failure fallback returns cleanly to Codex, Aider, or local sovereign lanes.
capabilities.rollback_or_disable_path: Demote OpenHands back to research-only status, remove it from preferred lane-selection output, and keep its artifacts as devstack-only pilot evidence.
capabilities.archive_instructions: Archive OpenHands-specific onboarding or experiment notes that are not needed for later pilot replay or rejection rationale.
notes: OpenHands is a bounded sidecar candidate, not a core stack replacement.
Any worker-lane adoption must remain subordinate to Athanor routing and sidecar retention policy.

capabilities.label: Letta Memory Plane
capabilities.runtime_target: Athanor memory-plane, context retention, and cross-session state governance
acceptance_criteria: Letta is used as an explicit memory plane, not a hidden parallel truth system.
Memory retention boundaries are compatible with Athanor privacy, sovereignty, and artifact-topology rules.
Cross-session state can be audited, replayed, and pruned under current governance rules.
Memory use improves operator continuity or agent quality on measured tasks.
capabilities.rollback_or_disable_path: Disable the pilot memory adapter and keep durable context on the current registry, packet, and repo-doc stack only.
capabilities.archive_instructions: Archive memory-plane comparison notes or prompt-state experiments that do not become part of the accepted memory contract.
notes: Letta is a memory-plane candidate only if it improves continuity without becoming another truth surface.

capabilities.label: Agent Governance Toolkit Policy Plane
capabilities.runtime_target: Athanor agent governance, trust, identity, and protocol-policy enforcement
acceptance_criteria: AGT can enforce or enrich existing Athanor policy classes without duplicating the whole control plane.
Trust, identity, and protocol governance produce measurable guardrail value for autonomous mutation or protocol bridging.
Governance events remain auditable through current operator-stream and decision-record surfaces.
Disabling the integration cleanly returns control to native Athanor policy enforcement.
source_safe_remaining: Do not draft a live Athanor adapter on the current single-scenario bundle.
Only reopen this lane if a second protocol-boundary scenario shows non-duplicative value over the native approval matrix without widening permissions.
capabilities.rollback_or_disable_path: Remove the AGT adapter or policy bridge and fall back to the existing Athanor approval, routing, and failure-governance contracts.
capabilities.archive_instructions: Archive protocol-governance comparison notes and unused bridge experiments that do not become part of the adopted governance layer.
notes: AGT is a policy-plane candidate, not a control-plane replacement.
The first implementation slice should stay narrow and protocol-boundary focused.
The current benchmark bundle shows a narrow bridge that adds only an explanatory policy verdict and stricter blocked actions.
The 2026-04-12 manual review concluded that this single approval-held-mutation scenario is still too redundant to justify a live adapter or release-tier advancement.
